note: Published limiting dimensions for each gauge. Suez has no length limit and its beam limit trades against draft — the figures here are the maximum-draft case. Malaccamax is a depth constraint, not a canal rule.

canals.name: Panamax
canals.note: The gauge that named a ship type. Air draft 57.91 m under the Bridge of the Americas.

canals.name: Neopanamax
canals.note: The expanded locks that reshaped world trade routes in 2016. Same 57.91 m air draft.

canals.name: Suezmax
canals.note: No length limit; beam trades against draft (up to 77.5 m at reduced draft — the megamax container ships transit this way). Figures shown are the maximum-draft case. Air draft 68 m.

canals.name: Malaccamax
canals.what: Strait of Malacca — depth of the Asia–Europe shortcut
canals.note: A geographic depth limit of roughly 25 m, not a rulebook. The half-million-tonne ULCCs failed it and detoured via Lombok.

canals.name: Seawaymax
canals.note: The small gauge that shapes every Great Lakes ship. Air draft 35.5 m.

canals.name: Chinamax
canals.note: A port standard rather than a waterway — the envelope the 400,000 t Valemax fleet was designed to fill exactly.
